reason: Both answers hit all the key points: reframe the comparison axis, weaponize the incumbent's breadth as complexity/cost, pick a metric/segment to dominate, target buyers who don't care about the checklist, and get ahead of the RFP/bake-off. Both are well-organized, concise, and correct. Answer A adds a nice "concede loudly" point and a useful closing caveat about needing to identify the segment first; Answer B has a slightly crisper category-reframing opener and buyer-targeting point. Neither is clearly more actionable or more correct than the other — the differences are stylistic, not substantive.
